newsSinceJun6: No material new fundamentals since June 6. Housekeeping only: $0.17 quarterly dividend (record date June 8, payable June 22) and 8-K setting the June 25 annual stockholder meeting; small former-officer insider sale June 4 at ~$63.19. The Raymond James Strong Buy upgrade and the Q1 beat (EPS $0.98 vs $0.78, ~$1.7B revenue, FY26 guidance reiterated at $7.5-8.1B sales / $4.50-5.00 EPS) all predate June 6. Data-center/AI-driven utilities demand commentary is supportive but stale (Q1 call). Today's +2.15% is flow/sector drift, below the 5% threshold and not tied to any new catalyst.

sources.label: StockTitan - Terex 8-K sets June 25, 2026 annual meeting
Simply Wall St - Terex analyst upgrade and margin outlook
Yahoo Finance - Terex Q1 2026 earnings call summary

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 90: no new evidence since June 6 strengthens or weakens the latent AI-node pathway (lifting/material handling into AI-driven industrial construction); today's +2.15% and YTD +16.4% are modest enough that residual alpha is roughly unchanged, consistent with yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Utilities and aerials demand tied to U.S. grid expansion and data-center construction gives Terex a real, still under-narrated AI-infrastructure pathway, with FY26 guidance ($7.5-8.1B sales, $4.50-5.00 EPS) reiterated and Raymond James at Strong Buy.
verdicts.neutral: A diversified machinery cyclical at ~$7.3B cap trading near its 52-week high; the AI/data-center link is one demand stream among many, and nothing since June 6 changes the strict-screen evidence either way.
verdicts.bear: Premium P/E for a cyclical near highs leaves little cushion if non-residential construction or aerials demand rolls over; the AI exposure is indirect and could be discounted quickly in a capex slowdown.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings and any quantified data-center/grid backlog disclosure in the utilities and aerials segments.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical non-residential construction downturn compressing aerials/cranes demand and multiple, swamping the indirect AI-infrastructure tailwind.
